query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "multi-lcb: extending livecodebench to multiple programming languages" computer science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) by investigating the same underlying mechanism (catastrophic forgetting during fine-tuning) and domain (multilingual foundation models) as the user's question, even though it uses machine translation as a testbed rather than code generation. It provides essential empirical baselines and conditions for knowledge retention in multilingual LLMs, which is a canonical prerequisite for any study extending benchmarks like LiveCodeBench to multiple programming l
